addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Introduction 6 The Verilog-to-Routing (VTR) project is a world-wide collaborative effort to provide an open-source framework for conducting FPGA architecture and CAD research and development. 7 The VTR design flow takes as input a Verilog description of a digital circuit, and a description of the target FPGA architecture. 8 It then performs: 9 * Elaboration, Synthesis & Partial Mapping (PARMYS) 10 * Logic Optimization & Technology Mapping (ABC) 11 * Packing, Placement, Routing & Timing Analysis (VPR) 12 13 to generate FPGA speed and area results. 14 VTR includes a set of benchmark designs known to work with the design flow. 15 16 VTR can also produce [FASM](https://fasm.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) to program some commercial FPGAs (via [Symbiflow](https://chipsalliance.org/announcement/2022/02/18/chips-alliance-forms-f4pga-workgroup-to-accelerate-adoption-of-open-source-fpga-tooling/)) 17 18 | Placement (carry-chains highlighted) | Critical Path | 19 | ------------------------------------ | ------------- | 20 | | | 21 22 | Logical Connections | Routing Utilization | 23 | ------------------- | ------------------- | 24 | | | 25 26 27 ## Syst.}, 47 year={2025} 48 } 49 ``` 50 51 ## Download 52 For most users of VTR (rather than active developers) you should download the [latest official VTR release](https://verilogtorouting.org/download), which has been fully regression tested. 53 54 ## Building 55 On unix-like systems run `make` from the root VTR directory. 56 57 For more details see the [building instructions](BUILDING.md). 58 59 #### Docker 60 We provide a Dockerfile that sets up all the necessary packages for VTR to run. 61 For more details see [here](dev/DOCKER_DEPLOY.md). 62 63 ## Mailing Lists 64 If you have questions, or want to keep up-to-date with VTR, consider joining our mailing lists: 65 66 [VTR-Announce](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/vtr-announce): VTR release announcements (low traffic) 67 68 [VTR-Users](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/vtr-users): Discussions about using VTR 69 70 [VTR-Devel](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/vtr-devel): Discussions about VTR development 71 72 [VTR-Commits](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/vtr-commits): VTR revision control commits 73 74 ## Development 75 This is the development trunk for the Verilog-to-Routing project. 76 Unlike the nicely packaged releases that we create, you are working with code in a constant state of flux. 77 You should expect that the tools are not always stable and that more work is needed to get the flow to run. 78 79 For new developers, please follow the [quickstart guide](https://docs.verilogtorouting.org/en/latest/quickstart/). 80 81 We follow a feature branch flow, where you create a new branch for new code, test it, measure its Quality of Results, and eventually produce a pull request for review by other developers. # This is a simple wrapper which hides cmake (for convenience, and from non-expert end users).
#
# It supports the targets:
#   'make'           - builds everything (all libraries/executables)
#   'make clean'     - removes generated build objects/libraries/executables etc.
#   'make distclean' - will clean everything including the cmake generated build files
#
# All other targets (e.g. 'make vpr') are passed to the cmake generated makefile
# and processed according to the CMakeLists.txt.
#
# To perform a debug build use:
#   'make BUILD_TYPE=debug'
#
# To enable debugging verbose messages as well use:
#
#   'make BUILD_TYPE=debug VERBOSE=1'

# Build type
# Possible values (not case sensitive):
#    release            #Build with compiler optimization (Default)
#    RelWithDebInfo     #Build with debug info and compiler optimizations
#    debug              #Build with debug info and no compiler optimization
# Possible suffixes:
#    _pgo               #Perform a 2-stage build with profile-guided compiler optimization
#    _strict            #Build VPR with warnings treated as errors
BUILD_TYPE ?= release

#Debugging verbosity enable
VERBOSE ?= 0

#Convert to lower case for consistency
BUILD_TYPE := $(shell echo $(BUILD_TYPE) | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')

#Trim any _pgo or _strict in the build type name (since it would not match any of
#CMake's standard build types)
CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE := $(shell echo $(BUILD_TYPE) | sed 's/_\?pgo//' | sed 's/_\?strict//')

#Detect the operating system
UNAME_S := $(shell uname -s)

# Cmake generator has to be Ninja on MSVC
CMAKE_GEN = Unix Makefiles
ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
CMAKE_GEN = Ninja
# Msys2 can still use Linux gcc
ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
CMAKE_GEN = Unix Makefiles
endif
endif

#Allows users to pass parameters to cmake
#  e.g. make CMAKE_PARAMS="-DVTR_ENABLE_SANITIZE=true"
override CMAKE_PARAMS := -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=$(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE) -G '${CMAKE_GEN}' ${CMAKE_PARAMS}

ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
# The curl path should be defined by user. Try to get one from system
CURL_PATH:=$(shell where curl.exe 2>nul | head -n 1)
# VCPKG root is a system variable env:VCPKG in power shell. User can override by using VCPKG_PATH when calling the makefile
VCPKG_CMAKE_PATH:=$(subst \,/,$(VCPKG_PATH))
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DWITH_PARMYS=OFF -DSLANG_SYSTEMVERILOG=OFF -DVTR_IPO_BUILD=OFF -DWITH_ABC=OFF
# Msys2 can still use Linux gcc
ifneq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DWGET="${CURL_PATH}" -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE="${VCPKG_CMAKE_PATH}/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake" -DVCPKG_TARGET_TRIPLET=x64-windows-release -DVCPKG_MANIFEST_MODE=OFF
#	override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DWITH_PARMYS=OFF -DSLANG_SYSTEMVERILOG=OFF -DVTR_IPO_BUILD=OFF -DWITH_ABC=OFF
endif
endif

#Are we doing a strict (i.e. warnings as errors) build?
ifneq (,$(findstring strict,$(BUILD_TYPE)))
	#Configure for strict build with VPR warning treated as errors
override CMAKE_PARAMS := -DCMAKE_COMPILE_WARNING_AS_ERROR=on ${CMAKE_PARAMS}
endif #Strict build type

#Enable verbosity
ifeq ($(VERBOSE),1)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := -DVTR_ENABLE_VERBOSE=on ${CMAKE_PARAMS}
endif

#`make ensure-gui` / `make ensure-headless` set VTR_GRAPHICS to force the
#graphics option. They delegate to the normal build rule below, so the PGO /
#build-dir / cmake handling is inherited rather than duplicated.
ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),on)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=on
endif
ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),off)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=off
endif

# -s : Suppresses makefile output (e.g. entering/leaving directories)
# --output-sync target : For parallel compilation ensure output for each target is synchronized (make version >= 4.0)
MAKEFLAGS := -s

ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
SOURCE_DIR := $(PWD)
else
SOURCE_DIR := $(shell powershell -NoProfile -Command "(Get-Location).Path")
SOURCE_DIR := $(subst \,/,$(SOURCE_DIR))
endif
else

# VTR Agent Guide

This file provides guidance to AI coding agents working in this repository.

## Quick Reference

Detailed guides are in `doc/agents/`. Read the relevant file before starting work in that area:

- `doc/agents/coding.md` — read before writing or reviewing any C++ code
- `doc/agents/codebase.md` — read before exploring or navigating the codebase
- `doc/agents/testing.md` — read before adding or running tests
- `doc/agents/build.md` — read before building or changing build configuration

## Project Overview

VTR (Verilog-to-Routing) is an open-source FPGA CAD framework. It takes Verilog as input and produces place-and-route results for FPGA architectures. The full flow runs:

1. **PARMYS** (Yosys-based) — Elaboration, synthesis, partial mapping (maintained here; Yosys itself is external)
2. **ABC** — Logic optimization and technology mapping (external, do not modify)
3. **VPR** — Packing, placement, routing, and timing analysis (**primary development target**)

A defining feature: VTR targets many different FPGA architectures, with the target architecture passed in as an XML file at runtime. CAD algorithms must therefore avoid hardcoding architecture-specific assumptions — the architecture is parsed at startup into data structures defined in `libarchfpga`.

See `doc/agents/codebase.md` for the full directory layout and VPR internal structure.

## Building

Always run `make` from the root directory — never invoke `cmake` directly. Build artifacts go in `build/`.

```shell
make          # full build
make -j8 vpr  # specific target with parallel jobs
```

Only build the project when prompted to. Suggest a build to the user once you reach a natural checkpoint (e.g., a self-contained change is complete and ready for review) or when you need see the compiler output.

See `doc/agents/build.md` for debug builds, CMake options, and build speed tips.

## Python Environment

Many scripts in `vtr_flow/scripts/` require the Python virtual environment. activate it with:

```shell
source .venv/bin/activate
```

## Running Tests

Only run tests when prompted to.

```shell
# Fast smoke test (~1 min)
./run_reg_test.py vtr_reg_basic

# Full functionality check (~20 min)
./run_reg_test.py vtr_reg_strong -j4

# All unit tests
make && make test
```

See `doc/agents/testing.md` for unit test details and how to add or update regression tests.

## AI Usage Policy

VTR requires that humans author all commits and pull requests. Do not create commits or open pull requests autonomously. When helping a developer, produce code and explanations for them to review, and let them decide what to commit and how to phrase the commit message. Do not add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or similar lines to commit messages.

content.content:
1	# VTR Codebase Layout
2	
3	## Top-Level Components
4	
5	| Directory | Purpose |
6	|-----------|---------|
7	| `vpr/` | Core FPGA CAD tool: pack, place, route, timing |
8	| `parmys/` | Yosys-based synthesis frontend (replaces Odin) |
9	| `odin_ii/` | Legacy synthesis frontend (disabled by default) |
10	| `abc/` | Logic optimization (external, do not modify directly) |
11	| `ace2/` | Activity estimation for power analysis |
12	| `libs/` | Shared libraries used by VPR and other tools |
13	| `vtr_flow/` | Flow scripts, architectures, benchmarks, regression tasks |
14	| `utils/` | Standalone utilities (FASM, VQM, route diagnostics) |
15	| `doc/` | RST documentation (served at docs.verilogtorouting.org) |
16	
17	## VPR Internal Structure (`vpr/src/`)
18	
19	| Subdirectory | Responsibility |
20	|--------------|---------------|
21	| `base/` | Core data structures: atom netlist, clustered netlist, device grid. **Start here when exploring data structures** — `vpr_context.h` defines all major contexts and `globals.h` exposes the global `g_vpr_ctx` accessor. |
22	| `pack/` | Technology mapping and clustering (LUT/FF packing into CLBs). Entry point: `pack.h` / `try_pack()` |
23	| `place/` | Simulated annealing placement. Entry point: `place.h` / `try_place()`. Subdirectories: `delay_model/` (placement delay models), `move_generators/` (SA move types), `timing/` (placement timing criticalities) |
24	| `route/` | Maze routing and connection router. Entry point: `route.h` / `route()`. Subdirectories: `router_lookahead/` (lookahead cost computation), `rr_graph_generation/` (building the routing resource graph) |
25	| `timing/` | Static timing analysis integration (uses Tatum library in `libs/EXTERNAL/libtatum`) |
26	| `analysis/` | Post-route analysis and reporting |
27	| `draw/` | OpenGL/EZGL graphical visualization |
28	| `noc/` | Network-on-Chip placement support |
29	| `analytical_place/` | Analytical placement (AP) engine. Entry point: `analytical_placement_flow.h` / `run_analytical_placement_flow()`. Subdirectories: `common/` (APNetlist and PartialPlacement data structures shared across stages), `global_placement/` (Stage 1: analytical solver + iterative partial legalization), `full_legalization/` (Stage 2: flat-to-clustered placement legalization), `detailed_placement/` (Stage 3: post-legalization quality optimization) |
30	| `power/` | Power estimation |
31	| `util/` | Shared VPR utilities (`vpr_utils.h`) used across multiple stages |
32	| `server/` | VPR server mode for external tool integration |
33	
34	## VPR Data Flow
35	
36	Understanding how data moves through VPR is essential before touching any stage. The traditional flow is:
37	
38	```
39	AtomNetlist → Prepacker → Packer → ClusteredNetlist → Placer → Router
40	```
41	
42	- **AtomNetlist** (`base/`) — primitive-level netlist parsed from the input circuit
43	- **Prepacker** (`pack/`) — groups atoms into molecules based on pack patterns; runs before both packing and analytical placement
44	- **Packer** (`pack/`) — clusters molecules into logic blocks, producing a **ClusteredNetlist**
45	- **Placer** (`place/`) — assigns each cluster to a physical tile on the device grid, populating **BlkLocRegistry**
46	- **Router** (`route/`) — routes all nets through the routing resource graph
47	
48	The analytical placement flow (`analytical_place/`) is an alternative path that integrates global placement before legalization into clusters, bypassing or wrapping the traditional packer depending on configuration.
49	
50	All major VPR data structures are held in contexts accessible via `g_vpr_ctx` (declared in `vpr/src/base/globals.h`). `vpr_context.h` in the same directory defines the full set of contexts and is the best starting point for understanding what data exists at each stage.
51	
52	## Shared Libraries (`libs/`)
53	
54	| Library | Purpose |
55	|---------|---------|
56	| `libarchfpga` | FPGA architecture XML parser and data structures (`arch_types.h`) |
57	| `libvtrutil` | Common utilities: logging, data structures (`vtr_vector`, `vtr_ndmatrix`, etc.) |
58	| `librrgraph` | Routing resource graph (RRG) data structures and serialization |
59	| `libpugiutil` | XML parsing helpers wrapping pugixml |
60	| `libvtrcapnproto` | Cap'n Proto binary serialization for RR graphs and router lookahead |
61	| `liblog` | Logging infrastructure |
62	| `librtlnumber` | RTL number representation |
63	
64	## External Subtrees (`libs/EXTERNAL/`)
65	
66	`libs/EXTERNAL/` contains code pulled in from upstream repositories via `git subtree`: `libargparse`, `libblifparse`, `libsdcparse`, and `libtatum`. file.content:
# VTR Codebase Layout

## Top-Level Components

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `vpr/` | Core FPGA CAD tool: pack, place, route, timing |
| `parmys/` | Yosys-based synthesis frontend (replaces Odin) |
| `odin_ii/` | Legacy synthesis frontend (disabled by default) |
| `abc/` | Logic optimization (external, do not modify directly) |
| `ace2/` | Activity estimation for power analysis |
| `libs/` | Shared libraries used by VPR and other tools |
| `vtr_flow/` | Flow scripts, architectures, benchmarks, regression tasks |
| `utils/` | Standalone utilities (FASM, VQM, route diagnostics) |
| `doc/` | RST documentation (served at docs.verilogtorouting.org) |

## VPR Internal Structure (`vpr/src/`)

| Subdirectory | Responsibility |
|--------------|---------------|
| `base/` | Core data structures: atom netlist, clustered netlist, device grid. **Start here when exploring data structures** — `vpr_context.h` defines all major contexts and `globals.h` exposes the global `g_vpr_ctx` accessor. |
| `pack/` | Technology mapping and clustering (LUT/FF packing into CLBs). Entry point: `pack.h` / `try_pack()` |
| `place/` | Simulated annealing placement. Entry point: `place.h` / `try_place()`. Subdirectories: `delay_model/` (placement delay models), `move_generators/` (SA move types), `timing/` (placement timing criticalities) |
| `route/` | Maze routing and connection router. Entry point: `route.h` / `route()`. Subdirectories: `router_lookahead/` (lookahead cost computation), `rr_graph_generation/` (building the routing resource graph) |
| `timing/` | Static timing analysis integration (uses Tatum library in `libs/EXTERNAL/libtatum`) |
| `analysis/` | Post-route analysis and reporting |
| `draw/` | OpenGL/EZGL graphical visualization |
| `noc/` | Network-on-Chip placement support |
| `analytical_place/` | Analytical placement (AP) engine. Entry point: `analytical_placement_flow.h` / `run_analytical_placement_flow()`. Subdirectories: `common/` (APNetlist and PartialPlacement data structures shared across stages), `global_placement/` (Stage 1: analytical solver + iterative partial legalization), `full_legalization/` (Stage 2: flat-to-clustered placement legalization), `detailed_placement/` (Stage 3: post-legalization quality optimization) |
| `power/` | Power estimation |
| `util/` | Shared VPR utilities (`vpr_utils.h`) used across multiple stages |
| `server/` | VPR server mode for external tool integration |

## VPR Data Flow

Understanding how data moves through VPR is essential before touching any stage. The traditional flow is:

```
AtomNetlist → Prepacker → Packer → ClusteredNetlist → Placer → Router
```

- **AtomNetlist** (`base/`) — primitive-level netlist parsed from the input circuit
- **Prepacker** (`pack/`) — groups atoms into molecules based on pack patterns; runs before both packing and analytical placement
- **Packer** (`pack/`) — clusters molecules into logic blocks, producing a **ClusteredNetlist**
- **Placer** (`place/`) — assigns each cluster to a physical tile on the device grid, populating **BlkLocRegistry**
- **Router** (`route/`) — routes all nets through the routing resource graph

The analytical placement flow (`analytical_place/`) is an alternative path that integrates global placement before legalization into clusters, bypassing or wrapping the traditional packer depending on configuration.

All major VPR data structures are held in contexts accessible via `g_vpr_ctx` (declared in `vpr/src/base/globals.h`). `vpr_context.h` in the same directory defines the full set of contexts and is the best starting point for understanding what data exists at each stage.

## Shared Libraries (`libs/`)

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `libarchfpga` | FPGA architecture XML parser and data structures (`arch_types.h`) |
| `libvtrutil` | Common utilities: logging, data structures (`vtr_vector`, `vtr_ndmatrix`, etc.) |
| `librrgraph` | Routing resource graph (RRG) data structures and serialization |
| `libpugiutil` | XML parsing helpers wrapping pugixml |
| `libvtrcapnproto` | Cap'n Proto binary serialization for RR graphs and router lookahead |
| `liblog` | Logging infrastructure |
| `librtlnumber` | RTL number representation |

## External Subtrees (`libs/EXTERNAL/`)

`libs/EXTERNAL/` contains code pulled in from upstream repositories via `git subtree`: `libargparse`, `libblifparse`, `libsdcparse`, and `libtatum`. Do not modify files under `libs/EXTERNAL/` directly — changes must be made in the upstream repository and then synced into VTR using `dev/external_subtrees.py`. The `abc/` directory at the repo root is similarly external and should not be modified directly.

## VTR Flow (`vtr_flow/`)

| Subdirectory | Content |
|---|---|
| `scripts/` | Python flow orchestration (`run_vtr_flow.py`, `run_vtr_task.py`, `parse_vtr_task.py`) |
| `tasks/regression_tests/` | Regression task configs and golden results |
| `arch/` | FPGA architecture XML files |
| `benchmarks/` | Benchmark circuits (Verilog, BLIF) including VTR, Koios, Titan |

# VTR Testing Guide

**Unit tests** are best for isolated APIs and data structures that can be exercised without a full VPR run (e.g., a new container or algorithm). **Regression tests** cover end-to-end behavior; because VPR's data structures require large input files to construct, most CAD algorithm changes are validated through regression tests rather than unit tests.

## Running the VTR Flow

Activate the Python virtual environment before running any scripts below:

```shell
source .venv/bin/activate
```

```shell
# Run a regression task
cd vtr_flow/tasks
../scripts/run_vtr_task.py regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/strong_timing

# Parse QoR results
../scripts/python_libs/vtr/parse_vtr_task.py regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/strong_timing

# Compare QoR across two runs
./vtr_flow/scripts/qor_compare.py parse_results_baseline.txt parse_results_new.txt -o comparison.xlsx
```

## Unit Tests

Unit tests use Catch2. Run them with:

```shell
# Build and run all unit tests
make && make test

# Run a specific unit test binary directly
cd build/vpr && ./test_vpr --list-tests
cd build/vpr && ./test_vpr connection_router binary_heap
```

Source-to-binary mapping:
- `vpr/test/` → `build/vpr/test_vpr`
- `libs/libarchfpga/test/` → `build/libs/libarchfpga/test_archfpga`
- `libs/libvtrutil/test/` → `build/libs/libvtrutil/test_vtrutil`
- `utils/fasm/test/` → `build/utils/fasm/test_fasm`

New unit tests go in the corresponding `test/` directory and are registered in the module's `CMakeLists.txt`.

## Adding a Regression Test

New features **must** include a regression test in `vtr_reg_strong` (small benchmarks, < 1 min runtime):

```shell
cd vtr_flow/tasks/regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong
mkdir -p strong_mytest/config
cp strong_timing/config/config.txt strong_mytest/config/.
# Edit the config: update the architecture file, circuit list, and VPR command-line flags for your test
# Then from vtr_flow/tasks/:
../scripts/run_vtr_task.py regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/strong_mytest
../scripts/python_libs/vtr/parse_vtr_task.py regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/strong_mytest -create_golden
../scripts/python_libs/vtr/parse_vtr_task.py regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/strong_mytest -check_golden
# Add path to vtr_flow/tasks/regression_tests/vtr_reg_strong/task_list.txt
```

content.thinking:
I now have a comprehensive understanding of the VTR project. Let me consolidate what I know and design the learning handbook.

## Project Summary

**VTR (Verilog-to-Routing)** is an open-source FPGA CAD framework. The full flow:
1. **PARMYS** (Yosys-based) — Elaboration, synthesis, partial mapping
2. **ABC** — Logic optimization and technology mapping (external)
3. **VPR** — Packing, placement, routing, and timing analysis (primary dev target)

**Architecture is data-driven** — passed as XML, parsed into `libarchfpga` structures.

### Top-level components:
- `vpr/` — Core FPGA CAD tool: pack, place, route, timing (primary target)
- `parmys/` — Yosys-based synthesis frontend (replaces Odin)
- `odin_ii/` — Legacy synthesis frontend (disabled by default)
- `abc/` — Logic optimization (external)
- `ace2/` — Activity estimation for power analysis
- `libs/` — Shared libraries (libarchfpga, libvtrutil, librrgraph, libpugiutil, libvtrcapnproto, liblog, librtlnumber, libvqm)
- `libs/EXTERNAL/` — External subtrees (libargparse, libblifparse, libsdcparse, libtatum, libpugixml, capnproto, libezgl, etc.)
- `vtr_flow/` — Flow scripts, architectures, benchmarks, regression tasks
- `utils/` — Standalone utilities (FASM, VQM, route diagnostics)
- `doc/` — RST documentation

### VPR internal structure (`vpr/src/`):
- `base/` — Core data structures: atom netlist, clustered netlist, device grid, contexts (`vpr_context.h`, `globals.h`, `vpr_api.h`, `vpr_types.h`, `main.cpp`)
- `pack/` — Tech mapping & clustering. Entry: `pack.h` / `try_pack()`. Internal: `prepack.h`, `greedy_clusterer.h`, `cluster_legalizer.h`, `pb_type_graph.h`, `cluster_router.h`, `atom_pb_bimap.h`
- `place/` — Simulated annealing placement. Entry: `place.h` / `try_place()`. Subdirs: `delay_model/`, `move_generators/`, timing/
- `route/` — Maze routing & connection router. Entry: `route.h` / `route()`. Subdirs: `router_lookahead/`, `rr_graph_generation/`
- `timing/` — Static timing analysis (Tatum)
- `analysis/` — Post-route analysis & reporting
- `draw/` — OpenGL/EZGL visualization
- `noc/` — Network-on-Chip placement
- `analytical_place/` — Analytical placement (AP) engine
- `power/` — Power estimation
- `util/` — VPR utilities
- `server/` — VPR server mode

### VPR Data Flow:
```
AtomNetlist → Prepacker → Packer → ClusteredNetlist → Placer → Router
```
- AtomNetlist (`base/`) — primitive-level netlist

input.rationale: VTR 是一个大型 FPGA CAD 框架，核心是 VPR（打包/布局/布线/时序），其设计灵魂是「架构驱动」——目标 FPGA 以 XML 描述、在启动时解析为 libarchfpga 数据结构。因此学习路线必须先让读者建立全局观，再深入架构解析，然后才是核心数据结构与三大 CAD 阶段。本手册分为 9 个单元、34 篇讲义：第 1 单元（入门）从零开始建立项目定位、构建运行、目录地图与全流程跑通；第 2 单元专攻架构 XML 与物理类型/器件网格；第 3 单元讲透 Netlist 家族与 VprContext 全局状态、以及 vpr_api 主流程编排；第 4–7 单元按真实数据流 AtomNetlist→Prepack→Packer→ClusteredNetlist→Placer→Router→Timing 顺序逐阶段深入；第 8 单元覆盖分析式布局、NoC、功耗、图形、Server/并行等高级扩展；第 9 单元收尾于共享库、测试与回归评估。讲义依赖按数据流自底向上编排，保证读者每篇都站在前序知识之上；每篇只解决一个主题、引用 1–6 个经确认的真实源码文件，并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。

units.title: 走进 VTR：定位、构建与全流程运行

lectures.title: VTR 是什么：项目定位与设计理念
lectures.topic: 介绍 VTR 作为开源 FPGA CAD 框架的整体定位、PARMYS→ABC→VPR 三段式流程，以及「架构驱动、不硬编码架构假设」这一核心设计理念。
learning_goals: 理解 VTR 解决什么问题、输入输出是什么
掌握 PARMYS、ABC、VPR 三段流程各自职责
理解架构 XML 在运行时才确定目标 FPGA 的设计哲学
minimal_modules: 三段式 CAD 流程总览
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README.md 与 AGENTS.md，用一段话写出 VTR 的输入、三段流程与最终产物；并列出 VTR 支持的两到三个目标 FPGA 架构文件来源。

lectures.title: 构建与运行 VTR
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用根目录 Makefile 包装的 CMake 构建系统编译 VTR，包括构建类型、并行编译、常见编译选项。
learning_goals: 掌握 make / make vpr 的标准构建命令
理解 Makefile 如何包装 CMake 以及构建产物位置
知道 release/debug/strict/pgo 等构建类型区别
minimal_modules: Makefile 包装层的工作原理
lectures.practice_task: 在本地克隆后运行 make -j8 vpr 完成最小构建，记录构建产物所在的 build/ 目录路径，并尝试用 BUILD_TYPE=debug 重新构建一次。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构与组件地图
lectures.topic: 梳理 VTR 顶层目录（vpr、parmys、odin_ii、abc、ace2、libs、vtr_flow、utils、doc）与 VPR 内部子目录（base、pack、place、route、timing 等）的职责。
learning_goals: 看懂顶层每个目录的用途
掌握 VPR/src 各子目录的责任划分
能根据要解决的问题快速定位目录
minimal_modules: 外部子树 libs/EXTERNAL 规则
lectures.practice_task: 参照 doc/agents/codebase.md 绘制一张 VTR 顶层与 vpr/src 的目录职责速查表，并标注哪些目录属于外部不可直接修改的代码。

lectures.title: 一键跑通 VTR 全流程
lectures.topic: 使用 vtr_flow/scripts/run_vtr_flow.py 把一个电路与架构跑通完整流程，理解 VTR_STAGES 与 Verilog 到布线结果的端到端链路。
learning_goals: 会用 run_vtr_flow.py 跑通一个完整设计
理解 odin/parmys/abc/vpr 各阶段产物
知道如何在指定阶段开始或结束
lectures.practice_task: 用 doc/src/quickstart 中的 blink.v 与一个示例架构文件运行 run_vtr_flow.py，观察并记录每一阶段生成的中间文件名。

lectures.title: VPR 命令行与参数体系
lectures.topic: 通过 read_options.h 与 main.cpp 理解 VPR 如何用 libargparse 解析命令行，建立对文件名、阶段动作（stage action）、各类选项结构体的整体认识。
learning_goals: 理解 t_options 与 e_stage_action 的设计
掌握 VPR 常用命令行参数
了解 argparse 选项的注册方式
lectures.practice_task: 运行 vpr --help（或查看 read_options 实现）列出与打包、布局、布线相关的三组关键参数，并说明每组的默认值来源。

lectures.title: FPGA 架构 XML 格式与解析入口
lectures.topic: 讲解 VTR 如何用 xml_read_arch 把架构 XML 解析为 t_arch 与物理/逻辑块类型集合，理解架构文件对布局布线行为的决定性影响。
learning_goals: 理解架构 XML 的整体结构
掌握 xml_read_arch 的输入输出
了解解析与校验的关系
minimal_modules: 架构 XML 顶层元素
lectures.practice_task: 阅读一个示例架构 XML（如 vtr_flow/arch 下任意文件）并对照 read_xml_arch_file 实现，列出该架构定义的器件类型、开关盒与线段。

lectures.title: 物理类型数据结构 physical_types
lectures.topic: 深入 physical_types.h 中的 t_physical_tile_type、t_logical_block_type、t_pb_type、t_arch、t_arch_switch_inf 等核心结构，建立对 FPGA 物理与逻辑建模的认识。
learning_goals: 区分物理块类型与逻辑块类型
理解 t_pb_type 描述的层次化逻辑块
掌握 t_arch 顶层结构
lectures.practice_task: 在 physical_types.h 中定位 t_physical_tile_type 与 t_logical_block_type，画出二者通过 equivalent_sites 关联的关系图。

lectures.title: 器件网格 DeviceGrid 的生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 DeviceGrid（device_grid.h）如何表示二维 FPGA 网格，以及 setup_grid.h 如何根据资源需求与 auto_layout 决定器件尺寸。
learning_goals: 理解 t_grid_tile 与 DeviceGrid 的表示
掌握 create_device_grid 的多种重载
了解 auto_layout 宽高比与目标利用率
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 vpr_create_device_grid（vpr_api.cpp）并跟踪它如何调用 setup_grid 的 create_device_grid，写出网格生成的调用链。

units.title: 核心数据结构与上下文体系

lectures.title: Netlist 泛型基类
lectures.topic: 讲解 netlist.h 的 Netlist<BlockId,PortId,PinId,NetId> 模板基类，它是 AtomNetlist 与 ClusteredNetlist 的共同抽象。
learning_goals: 理解 Netlist 模板的块/端口/引脚/线网四元模型
掌握 StrongId 类型安全 ID 设计
了解 netlist 的增删查改接口
lectures.practice_task: 在 netlist.h 中找出 block/port/pin/net 四类实体各自的 ID 别名与主容器，并写一段伪代码说明如何由一个 pin 找到其所属 net。

lectures.title: AtomNetlist 原子级网表
lectures.topic: 讲解 AtomNetlist（atom_netlist.h）如何表示技术映射后的原子级电路（LUT/FF 等原语），以及如何从 BLIF 读入。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomNetlist 的原语模型与真值表
掌握从 BLIF 读入网表的入口
了解 LogicalModel 与原语的绑定
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪 read_blif 相关代码，写出从 .blif 文件到 AtomNetlist 构造的主要步骤，并指出真值表存储在哪个数据结构中。

lectures.title: ClusteredNetlist 聚簇网表
lectures.topic: 讲解 ClusteredNetlist（clustered_netlist.h）如何表示打包后的逻辑块级网表，它是布局布线阶段的输入。
learning_goals: 理解聚簇网表与原子网表的区别
掌握逻辑块与物理块的对应关系
了解打包产物的结构
lectures.practice_task: 对比 AtomNetlist 与 ClusteredNetlist 的主要差异，说明打包阶段把哪些信息从原子层提升到了逻辑块层。

lectures.title: VprContext 与全局状态管理
lectures.topic: 讲解 vpr_context.h 定义的各 Context（Atom/Device/Clustering/Placement/Routing/Timing）以及全局访问器 g_vpr_ctx，这是理解 VPR 所有阶段共享状态的关键。
learning_goals: 掌握 VprContext 聚合的子上下文
理解不可拷贝 Context 的 mutable/immutable 访问模式
了解 g_vpr_ctx 全局访问约定
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_context.h 中列出 DeviceContext、AtomContext、TimingContext 各自持有哪些关键成员，并说明为何 Context 禁止拷贝。

lectures.title: 主流程编排 vpr_api
lectures.topic: 讲解 vpr_api.h/.cpp 如何编排初始化、打包、布局布线、分析、释放的完整生命周期，是连接所有阶段的主动脉。
learning_goals: 掌握 vpr_init/vpr_flow/vpr_free_all 的职责
理解 vpr_pack_flow/vpr_place_flow/vpr_route_flow/vpr_analysis_flow 的分派
了解 t_vpr_setup 配置对象的作用
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中跟踪 vpr_flow 的实现，画出从 init 到 analysis 的阶段调用顺序图，并标注每个阶段读写哪个 Context。

lectures.title: Packing 总览与 PB 类型图
lectures.topic: 讲解 try_pack（pack.h）的整体流程，以及 pb_type_graph.h 如何把架构中的 t_pb_type 层次展开为可布线的 PB 图，为聚类提供合法化依据。
learning_goals: 理解打包要解决的问题与输入输出
掌握 PB 图的层次化结构
了解 try_pack 的主要步骤
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 vpr_pack（vpr_api.cpp）与 try_pack 的签名，列出打包前需要预先准备好的输入对象（如 Prepacker、PreClusterTimingManager 等）。

lectures.title: Prepacker 与打包分子
lectures.topic: 讲解 Prepacker（prepack.h）如何根据 pack pattern（如进位链）把原子组合成「打包分子」，作为聚簇的基本单元。
learning_goals: 理解 pack pattern 与分子的概念
掌握 Prepacker 的输入输出
了解分子对聚类合法性的影响
lectures.practice_task: 在 prepack.cpp 中找到构建分子的核心函数，说明进位链类 pack pattern 如何把多个原子绑定为一个分子。

lectures.title: 贪心聚簇器 GreedyClusterer
lectures.topic: 讲解 GreedyClusterer 如何通过种子选择与候选增益评估，一次生成一个合法簇，把分子聚类成逻辑块。
learning_goals: 理解贪心聚簇的增益评估
掌握种子选择与候选选择分工
了解 APPack/吸引力组对聚类质量的提升
lectures.practice_task: 对比 greedy_seed_selector 与 greedy_candidate_selector 的职责，写出一次聚簇迭代中二者的协作顺序。

lectures.title: 聚簇合法化与簇内布线
lectures.topic: 讲解 cluster_legalizer 与 cluster_router 如何验证簇内连线的可布线性，并基于 lb_type_rr_graph 进行簇内布线。
learning_goals: 理解簇内布线合法性的判定
掌握 lb_type_rr_graph 的作用
了解聚簇合法化失败的处理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 cluster_router 与 lb_type_rr_graph，说明簇内布线使用的是什么图结构，并指出合法化失败时聚簇器如何回退。

lectures.title: 布局总览与模拟退火框架
lectures.topic: 讲解 try_place（place.h）、Placer 与 annealer 构成的模拟退火布局框架，理解退火温度、代价函数与接受准则。
learning_goals: 理解模拟退火布局的目标
掌握 Placer 与 annealer 的分工
了解退火调度与终止条件
lectures.practice_task: 在 placer.h/annealer.h 中找到退火主循环，记录每一次尝试需要评估的代价项，并说明温度如何影响接受概率。

lectures.title: 移动生成器
lectures.topic: 讲解 move_generators 如何产生不同类型的布局移动（均匀、关键路径优先、RL、质心等），决定退火每一步如何改变布局。
learning_goals: 理解移动生成器在退火中的角色
掌握不同移动类型的策略
了解 RL 移动生成器的学习机制
lectures.practice_task: 列出 move_generators 目录下所有移动生成器类型，比较 simpleRL 与 critical_uniform 两种策略的选择依据。

lectures.title: 布局代价与延迟模型
lectures.topic: 讲解布局延迟模型（delta/simple/override）如何估算布局代价，以及 move_transactions 与 compressed_grid 如何支持增量代价计算。
learning_goals: 理解布局代价中的线长与时序项
掌握延迟模型的层次与切换
了解增量代价与事务回滚
lectures.practice_task: 对比 simple_delay_model 与 delta_delay_model 的精度与开销，说明 move_transactions 如何在一次退火移动中提交或回滚代价。

lectures.title: 路由资源图 RR Graph
lectures.topic: 讲解路由资源图（RR Graph）如何描述器件上所有可布线的节点与边，以及 rr_graph_generation 如何在给定通道宽度下构建该图。
learning_goals: 理解 RR 节点/边的类型与含义
掌握 rr_graph_view 的只读访问
了解通道宽度与图构建的关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 rr_types.h 或 rr_node.h 中列出 RR 节点的所有类型，并跟踪 vpr_create_rr_graph 说明通道宽度如何影响图的规模。

lectures.title: 连接路由器与堆结构
lectures.topic: 讲解 Connection Router 如何用基于堆的 Dijkstra/maze 搜索为每个连接寻找最低代价路径，以及 d_ary_heap 等堆实现。
learning_goals: 理解迷宫布线与堆的关系
掌握代价函数的现时与前瞻项
了解不同堆实现对性能的影响
lectures.practice_task: 在 connection_router.h 中找到主搜索循环，说明每个 RR 节点入堆时考虑的代价由哪几部分组成，并比较不同堆类型的选择入口（heap_type.h）。

lectures.title: 基于连接的路由与路由树
lectures.topic: 讲解 route()（route.h）如何迭代布线、Connection_based_routing 如何做增量重布线，以及 route_tree 如何复用已布线段（AIR 算法）。
learning_goals: 理解迭代布线与拥塞化解
掌握连接级增量重布线机制
了解路由树剪枝与复用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 connection_based_routing.h 中关于 forcible_reroute 与 lower_bound_connection_delay 的注释，说明何时会触发对某连接的强制重布线。

lectures.title: 路由器前瞻 Router Lookahead
lectures.topic: 讲解 router_lookahead 如何提供「到达目标还剩多少代价」的估算，使迷宫搜索朝目标定向扩展，提升布线速度与质量。
learning_goals: 理解前瞻代价的作用
掌握 cost_map 等前瞻类型
了解前瞻的预计算与序列化
lectures.practice_task: 在 router_lookahead.h 中列出可选的前瞻类型枚举（e_router_lookahead），并说明 cost_map 类前瞻相比 simple 类的精度与开销权衡。

units.title: 时序分析与布线后评估

lectures.title: 时序图构建与 Tatum 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 如何把网表与延迟构建为 Tatum 时序图，TimingContext 如何持有图与约束，以及时序分析的触发时机。
learning_goals: 理解时序图的节点与边
掌握 Tatum 库的集成方式
了解 TimingContext 的组成
lectures.practice_task: 在 timing_graph_builder.h 中找出构建时序图所需输入，并说明 TimingContext 中的 graph 与 constraints 何时被赋值。

lectures.title: 时序约束、延迟计算与布线后报告
lectures.topic: 讲解 SDC 约束读入、各阶段延迟计算器（PreCluster/Placement/Routing）、net_delay，以及布线后分析与时序报告。
learning_goals: 理解 SDC 约束如何影响时序
掌握不同阶段延迟计算器分工
了解布线后分析与关键路径报告
lectures.practice_task: 比较 PreClusterDelayCalculator 与 RoutingDelayCalculator 的输入来源，说明 net_delay 在二者之间的传递方式，并指出关键路径报告由哪个文件生成。

lectures.title: 分析式布局引擎
lectures.topic: 讲解 analytical_place 如何用解析求解器在合法化之前先做全局布局（APNetlist→全局布局→合法化→详细布局），作为传统打包布局的替代路径。
learning_goals: 理解分析式布局的三阶段流程
掌握 APNetlist 与 PartialPlacement 结构
了解与传统打包流程的关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中找到分析式布局的入口分派，列出其三个子阶段对应的目录与职责。

lectures.title: NoC 布局与路由
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 对片上网络（NoC）的支持：noc_storage 建模 NoC 拓扑、noc_traffic_flows 描述流量、noc_routing 选择路由算法。
learning_goals: 理解 NoC 在 FPGA 中的建模
掌握流量与路由的表示
了解 NoC 对布局布线的影响
lectures.practice_task: 列出 noc 目录下支持的路由算法，并说明 noc_traffic_flows 描述的流量如何驱动 NoC 路由决策。

lectures.title: 功耗分析
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 的功耗估算流程：power.h 顶层接口、power_components 组件分解、与 ace2 活动性估计的配合。
learning_goals: 理解功耗估算的输入（活动性、CMOS 工艺）
掌握功耗组件分解结构
了解 ace2 的角色
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中找到 vpr_power_estimation 的调用点，列出功耗估算所需的输入数据来源（活动性文件、CMOS 工艺文件）。

lectures.title: 图形可视化
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 基于 EZGL/OpenGL 的图形界面（draw/），如何在布局布线过程中实时观察器件、网表与布线结果。
learning_goals: 理解图形界面的启用条件与构建开关
掌握可可视化的对象层次
了解断点与交互调试
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 doc/src/vpr/graphics.rst 与 draw.h，说明如何启用图形（make ensure-gui），并列出可高亮显示的三类对象。

lectures.title: Server 模式与并行布线
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 的 server 模式（供外部工具集成）与多线程并行布线（ParallelNetlistRouter + multi_queue_d_ary_heap）。
learning_goals: 理解 server 模式的通信模型
掌握并行布线的任务划分
了解多队列堆对并发的支持
lectures.practice_task: 比较 SerialNetlistRouter 与 ParallelNetlistRouter 的差异，说明 multi_queue_d_ary_heap 如何让多线程并行扩展堆搜索。

units.title: 共享库、测试与工程实践

lectures.title: 共享库 libvtrutil 与 VPR 工具
lectures.topic: 讲解 libvtrutil 提供的通用数据结构（vtr_vector、vtr_ndmatrix、vtr_cache、日志/错误）以及 vpr_utils 如何在多阶段间共享辅助逻辑。
learning_goals: 掌握 libvtrutil 的核心容器与工具
理解 StrongId 索引容器的设计
了解 vpr_utils 的跨阶段用途
minimal_modules: 通用容器与 ID 容器
lectures.practice_task: 在 libvtrutil/src 中挑出 vtr_vector 与 vtr_ndmatrix，说明它们如何用 StrongId 作下标，并写出 vpr_utils 中一个被多处调用的辅助函数。

lectures.title: 单元测试体系 Catch2
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR/libarchfpga/libvtrutil 的 Catch2 单元测试组织方式，以及如何构建运行单个测试用例。
learning_goals: 掌握各模块测试二进制的源到目标映射
理解 Catch2 测试的组织与命名
了解如何新增单元测试
minimal_modules: 测试目录与二进制映射
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make && make test，并执行 build/vpr/test_vpr --list-tests 列出连接路由相关的测试用例，挑选一个读懂其断言逻辑。

lectures.title: 回归测试与 QoR 评估
lectures.topic: 讲解 vtr_flow 的回归任务体系（run_vtr_task.py）、黄金结果比对、以及如何用 parse_vtr_task 与 qor_compare 评估质量结果（QoR）。
learning_goals: 掌握回归任务的配置与运行
理解黄金结果的生成与校验
了解 QoR 对比与 PR 流程
lectures.practice_task: 按 testing.md 步骤复制一个 vtr_reg_strong 任务配置，运行并生成黄金结果，再用 qor_compare 对比两次运行的 QoR。

data.head: c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19
data.rationale: VTR 是一个大型 FPGA CAD 框架，核心是 VPR（打包/布局/布线/时序），其设计灵魂是「架构驱动」——目标 FPGA 以 XML 描述、在启动时解析为 libarchfpga 数据结构。因此学习路线必须先让读者建立全局观，再深入架构解析，然后才是核心数据结构与三大 CAD 阶段。本手册分为 9 个单元、34 篇讲义：第 1 单元（入门）从零开始建立项目定位、构建运行、目录地图与全流程跑通；第 2 单元专攻架构 XML 与物理类型/器件网格；第 3 单元讲透 Netlist 家族与 VprContext 全局状态、以及 vpr_api 主流程编排；第 4–7 单元按真实数据流 AtomNetlist→Prepack→Packer→ClusteredNetlist→Placer→Router→Timing 顺序逐阶段深入；第 8 单元覆盖分析式布局、NoC、功耗、图形、Server/并行等高级扩展；第 9 单元收尾于共享库、测试与回归评估。讲义依赖按数据流自底向上编排，保证读者每篇都站在前序知识之上；每篇只解决一个主题、引用 1–6 个经确认的真实源码文件，并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。

units.title: 走进 VTR：定位、构建与全流程运行

lectures.title: VTR 是什么：项目定位与设计理念
lectures.topic: 介绍 VTR 作为开源 FPGA CAD 框架的整体定位、PARMYS→ABC→VPR 三段式流程，以及「架构驱动、不硬编码架构假设」这一核心设计理念。
learning_goals: 理解 VTR 解决什么问题、输入输出是什么
掌握 PARMYS、ABC、VPR 三段流程各自职责
理解架构 XML 在运行时才确定目标 FPGA 的设计哲学
minimal_modules: 三段式 CAD 流程总览
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README.md 与 AGENTS.md，用一段话写出 VTR 的输入、三段流程与最终产物；并列出 VTR 支持的两到三个目标 FPGA 架构文件来源。

lectures.title: 构建与运行 VTR
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用根目录 Makefile 包装的 CMake 构建系统编译 VTR，包括构建类型、并行编译、常见编译选项。
learning_goals: 掌握 make / make vpr 的标准构建命令
理解 Makefile 如何包装 CMake 以及构建产物位置
知道 release/debug/strict/pgo 等构建类型区别
minimal_modules: Makefile 包装层的工作原理
lectures.practice_task: 在本地克隆后运行 make -j8 vpr 完成最小构建，记录构建产物所在的 build/ 目录路径，并尝试用 BUILD_TYPE=debug 重新构建一次。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构与组件地图
lectures.topic: 梳理 VTR 顶层目录（vpr、parmys、odin_ii、abc、ace2、libs、vtr_flow、utils、doc）与 VPR 内部子目录（base、pack、place、route、timing 等）的职责。
learning_goals: 看懂顶层每个目录的用途
掌握 VPR/src 各子目录的责任划分
能根据要解决的问题快速定位目录
minimal_modules: 外部子树 libs/EXTERNAL 规则
lectures.practice_task: 参照 doc/agents/codebase.md 绘制一张 VTR 顶层与 vpr/src 的目录职责速查表，并标注哪些目录属于外部不可直接修改的代码。

lectures.title: 一键跑通 VTR 全流程
lectures.topic: 使用 vtr_flow/scripts/run_vtr_flow.py 把一个电路与架构跑通完整流程，理解 VTR_STAGES 与 Verilog 到布线结果的端到端链路。
learning_goals: 会用 run_vtr_flow.py 跑通一个完整设计
理解 odin/parmys/abc/vpr 各阶段产物
知道如何在指定阶段开始或结束
lectures.practice_task: 用 doc/src/quickstart 中的 blink.v 与一个示例架构文件运行 run_vtr_flow.py，观察并记录每一阶段生成的中间文件名。

lectures.title: VPR 命令行与参数体系
lectures.topic: 通过 read_options.h 与 main.cpp 理解 VPR 如何用 libargparse 解析命令行，建立对文件名、阶段动作（stage action）、各类选项结构体的整体认识。
learning_goals: 理解 t_options 与 e_stage_action 的设计
掌握 VPR 常用命令行参数
了解 argparse 选项的注册方式
lectures.practice_task: 运行 vpr --help（或查看 read_options 实现）列出与打包、布局、布线相关的三组关键参数，并说明每组的默认值来源。

lectures.title: FPGA 架构 XML 格式与解析入口
lectures.topic: 讲解 VTR 如何用 xml_read_arch 把架构 XML 解析为 t_arch 与物理/逻辑块类型集合，理解架构文件对布局布线行为的决定性影响。
learning_goals: 理解架构 XML 的整体结构
掌握 xml_read_arch 的输入输出
了解解析与校验的关系
minimal_modules: 架构 XML 顶层元素
lectures.practice_task: 阅读一个示例架构 XML（如 vtr_flow/arch 下任意文件）并对照 read_xml_arch_file 实现，列出该架构定义的器件类型、开关盒与线段。

lectures.title: 物理类型数据结构 physical_types
lectures.topic: 深入 physical_types.h 中的 t_physical_tile_type、t_logical_block_type、t_pb_type、t_arch、t_arch_switch_inf 等核心结构，建立对 FPGA 物理与逻辑建模的认识。
learning_goals: 区分物理块类型与逻辑块类型
理解 t_pb_type 描述的层次化逻辑块
掌握 t_arch 顶层结构
lectures.practice_task: 在 physical_types.h 中定位 t_physical_tile_type 与 t_logical_block_type，画出二者通过 equivalent_sites 关联的关系图。

lectures.title: 器件网格 DeviceGrid 的生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 DeviceGrid（device_grid.h）如何表示二维 FPGA 网格，以及 setup_grid.h 如何根据资源需求与 auto_layout 决定器件尺寸。
learning_goals: 理解 t_grid_tile 与 DeviceGrid 的表示
掌握 create_device_grid 的多种重载
了解 auto_layout 宽高比与目标利用率
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 vpr_create_device_grid（vpr_api.cpp）并跟踪它如何调用 setup_grid 的 create_device_grid，写出网格生成的调用链。

units.title: 核心数据结构与上下文体系

lectures.title: Netlist 泛型基类
lectures.topic: 讲解 netlist.h 的 Netlist<BlockId,PortId,PinId,NetId> 模板基类，它是 AtomNetlist 与 ClusteredNetlist 的共同抽象。
learning_goals: 理解 Netlist 模板的块/端口/引脚/线网四元模型
掌握 StrongId 类型安全 ID 设计
了解 netlist 的增删查改接口
lectures.practice_task: 在 netlist.h 中找出 block/port/pin/net 四类实体各自的 ID 别名与主容器，并写一段伪代码说明如何由一个 pin 找到其所属 net。

lectures.title: AtomNetlist 原子级网表
lectures.topic: 讲解 AtomNetlist（atom_netlist.h）如何表示技术映射后的原子级电路（LUT/FF 等原语），以及如何从 BLIF 读入。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomNetlist 的原语模型与真值表
掌握从 BLIF 读入网表的入口
了解 LogicalModel 与原语的绑定
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪 read_blif 相关代码，写出从 .blif 文件到 AtomNetlist 构造的主要步骤，并指出真值表存储在哪个数据结构中。

lectures.title: ClusteredNetlist 聚簇网表
lectures.topic: 讲解 ClusteredNetlist（clustered_netlist.h）如何表示打包后的逻辑块级网表，它是布局布线阶段的输入。
learning_goals: 理解聚簇网表与原子网表的区别
掌握逻辑块与物理块的对应关系
了解打包产物的结构
lectures.practice_task: 对比 AtomNetlist 与 ClusteredNetlist 的主要差异，说明打包阶段把哪些信息从原子层提升到了逻辑块层。

lectures.title: VprContext 与全局状态管理
lectures.topic: 讲解 vpr_context.h 定义的各 Context（Atom/Device/Clustering/Placement/Routing/Timing）以及全局访问器 g_vpr_ctx，这是理解 VPR 所有阶段共享状态的关键。
learning_goals: 掌握 VprContext 聚合的子上下文
理解不可拷贝 Context 的 mutable/immutable 访问模式
了解 g_vpr_ctx 全局访问约定
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_context.h 中列出 DeviceContext、AtomContext、TimingContext 各自持有哪些关键成员，并说明为何 Context 禁止拷贝。

lectures.title: 主流程编排 vpr_api
lectures.topic: 讲解 vpr_api.h/.cpp 如何编排初始化、打包、布局布线、分析、释放的完整生命周期，是连接所有阶段的主动脉。
learning_goals: 掌握 vpr_init/vpr_flow/vpr_free_all 的职责
理解 vpr_pack_flow/vpr_place_flow/vpr_route_flow/vpr_analysis_flow 的分派
了解 t_vpr_setup 配置对象的作用
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中跟踪 vpr_flow 的实现，画出从 init 到 analysis 的阶段调用顺序图，并标注每个阶段读写哪个 Context。

lectures.title: Packing 总览与 PB 类型图
lectures.topic: 讲解 try_pack（pack.h）的整体流程，以及 pb_type_graph.h 如何把架构中的 t_pb_type 层次展开为可布线的 PB 图，为聚类提供合法化依据。
learning_goals: 理解打包要解决的问题与输入输出
掌握 PB 图的层次化结构
了解 try_pack 的主要步骤
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 vpr_pack（vpr_api.cpp）与 try_pack 的签名，列出打包前需要预先准备好的输入对象（如 Prepacker、PreClusterTimingManager 等）。

lectures.title: Prepacker 与打包分子
lectures.topic: 讲解 Prepacker（prepack.h）如何根据 pack pattern（如进位链）把原子组合成「打包分子」，作为聚簇的基本单元。
learning_goals: 理解 pack pattern 与分子的概念
掌握 Prepacker 的输入输出
了解分子对聚类合法性的影响
lectures.practice_task: 在 prepack.cpp 中找到构建分子的核心函数，说明进位链类 pack pattern 如何把多个原子绑定为一个分子。

lectures.title: 贪心聚簇器 GreedyClusterer
lectures.topic: 讲解 GreedyClusterer 如何通过种子选择与候选增益评估，一次生成一个合法簇，把分子聚类成逻辑块。
learning_goals: 理解贪心聚簇的增益评估
掌握种子选择与候选选择分工
了解 APPack/吸引力组对聚类质量的提升
lectures.practice_task: 对比 greedy_seed_selector 与 greedy_candidate_selector 的职责，写出一次聚簇迭代中二者的协作顺序。

lectures.title: 聚簇合法化与簇内布线
lectures.topic: 讲解 cluster_legalizer 与 cluster_router 如何验证簇内连线的可布线性，并基于 lb_type_rr_graph 进行簇内布线。
learning_goals: 理解簇内布线合法性的判定
掌握 lb_type_rr_graph 的作用
了解聚簇合法化失败的处理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 cluster_router 与 lb_type_rr_graph，说明簇内布线使用的是什么图结构，并指出合法化失败时聚簇器如何回退。

lectures.title: 布局总览与模拟退火框架
lectures.topic: 讲解 try_place（place.h）、Placer 与 annealer 构成的模拟退火布局框架，理解退火温度、代价函数与接受准则。
learning_goals: 理解模拟退火布局的目标
掌握 Placer 与 annealer 的分工
了解退火调度与终止条件
lectures.practice_task: 在 placer.h/annealer.h 中找到退火主循环，记录每一次尝试需要评估的代价项，并说明温度如何影响接受概率。

lectures.title: 移动生成器
lectures.topic: 讲解 move_generators 如何产生不同类型的布局移动（均匀、关键路径优先、RL、质心等），决定退火每一步如何改变布局。
learning_goals: 理解移动生成器在退火中的角色
掌握不同移动类型的策略
了解 RL 移动生成器的学习机制
lectures.practice_task: 列出 move_generators 目录下所有移动生成器类型，比较 simpleRL 与 critical_uniform 两种策略的选择依据。

lectures.title: 布局代价与延迟模型
lectures.topic: 讲解布局延迟模型（delta/simple/override）如何估算布局代价，以及 move_transactions 与 compressed_grid 如何支持增量代价计算。
learning_goals: 理解布局代价中的线长与时序项
掌握延迟模型的层次与切换
了解增量代价与事务回滚
lectures.practice_task: 对比 simple_delay_model 与 delta_delay_model 的精度与开销，说明 move_transactions 如何在一次退火移动中提交或回滚代价。

lectures.title: 路由资源图 RR Graph
lectures.topic: 讲解路由资源图（RR Graph）如何描述器件上所有可布线的节点与边，以及 rr_graph_generation 如何在给定通道宽度下构建该图。
learning_goals: 理解 RR 节点/边的类型与含义
掌握 rr_graph_view 的只读访问
了解通道宽度与图构建的关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 rr_types.h 或 rr_node.h 中列出 RR 节点的所有类型，并跟踪 vpr_create_rr_graph 说明通道宽度如何影响图的规模。

lectures.title: 连接路由器与堆结构
lectures.topic: 讲解 Connection Router 如何用基于堆的 Dijkstra/maze 搜索为每个连接寻找最低代价路径，以及 d_ary_heap 等堆实现。
learning_goals: 理解迷宫布线与堆的关系
掌握代价函数的现时与前瞻项
了解不同堆实现对性能的影响
lectures.practice_task: 在 connection_router.h 中找到主搜索循环，说明每个 RR 节点入堆时考虑的代价由哪几部分组成，并比较不同堆类型的选择入口（heap_type.h）。

lectures.title: 基于连接的路由与路由树
lectures.topic: 讲解 route()（route.h）如何迭代布线、Connection_based_routing 如何做增量重布线，以及 route_tree 如何复用已布线段（AIR 算法）。
learning_goals: 理解迭代布线与拥塞化解
掌握连接级增量重布线机制
了解路由树剪枝与复用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 connection_based_routing.h 中关于 forcible_reroute 与 lower_bound_connection_delay 的注释，说明何时会触发对某连接的强制重布线。

lectures.title: 路由器前瞻 Router Lookahead
lectures.topic: 讲解 router_lookahead 如何提供「到达目标还剩多少代价」的估算，使迷宫搜索朝目标定向扩展，提升布线速度与质量。
learning_goals: 理解前瞻代价的作用
掌握 cost_map 等前瞻类型
了解前瞻的预计算与序列化
lectures.practice_task: 在 router_lookahead.h 中列出可选的前瞻类型枚举（e_router_lookahead），并说明 cost_map 类前瞻相比 simple 类的精度与开销权衡。

units.title: 时序分析与布线后评估

lectures.title: 时序图构建与 Tatum 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 如何把网表与延迟构建为 Tatum 时序图，TimingContext 如何持有图与约束，以及时序分析的触发时机。
learning_goals: 理解时序图的节点与边
掌握 Tatum 库的集成方式
了解 TimingContext 的组成
lectures.practice_task: 在 timing_graph_builder.h 中找出构建时序图所需输入，并说明 TimingContext 中的 graph 与 constraints 何时被赋值。

lectures.title: 时序约束、延迟计算与布线后报告
lectures.topic: 讲解 SDC 约束读入、各阶段延迟计算器（PreCluster/Placement/Routing）、net_delay，以及布线后分析与时序报告。
learning_goals: 理解 SDC 约束如何影响时序
掌握不同阶段延迟计算器分工
了解布线后分析与关键路径报告
lectures.practice_task: 比较 PreClusterDelayCalculator 与 RoutingDelayCalculator 的输入来源，说明 net_delay 在二者之间的传递方式，并指出关键路径报告由哪个文件生成。

lectures.title: 分析式布局引擎
lectures.topic: 讲解 analytical_place 如何用解析求解器在合法化之前先做全局布局（APNetlist→全局布局→合法化→详细布局），作为传统打包布局的替代路径。
learning_goals: 理解分析式布局的三阶段流程
掌握 APNetlist 与 PartialPlacement 结构
了解与传统打包流程的关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中找到分析式布局的入口分派，列出其三个子阶段对应的目录与职责。

lectures.title: NoC 布局与路由
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 对片上网络（NoC）的支持：noc_storage 建模 NoC 拓扑、noc_traffic_flows 描述流量、noc_routing 选择路由算法。
learning_goals: 理解 NoC 在 FPGA 中的建模
掌握流量与路由的表示
了解 NoC 对布局布线的影响
lectures.practice_task: 列出 noc 目录下支持的路由算法，并说明 noc_traffic_flows 描述的流量如何驱动 NoC 路由决策。

lectures.title: 功耗分析
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 的功耗估算流程：power.h 顶层接口、power_components 组件分解、与 ace2 活动性估计的配合。
learning_goals: 理解功耗估算的输入（活动性、CMOS 工艺）
掌握功耗组件分解结构
了解 ace2 的角色
lectures.practice_task: 在 vpr_api.cpp 中找到 vpr_power_estimation 的调用点，列出功耗估算所需的输入数据来源（活动性文件、CMOS 工艺文件）。

lectures.title: 图形可视化
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 基于 EZGL/OpenGL 的图形界面（draw/），如何在布局布线过程中实时观察器件、网表与布线结果。
learning_goals: 理解图形界面的启用条件与构建开关
掌握可可视化的对象层次
了解断点与交互调试
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 doc/src/vpr/graphics.rst 与 draw.h，说明如何启用图形（make ensure-gui），并列出可高亮显示的三类对象。

lectures.title: Server 模式与并行布线
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR 的 server 模式（供外部工具集成）与多线程并行布线（ParallelNetlistRouter + multi_queue_d_ary_heap）。
learning_goals: 理解 server 模式的通信模型
掌握并行布线的任务划分
了解多队列堆对并发的支持
lectures.practice_task: 比较 SerialNetlistRouter 与 ParallelNetlistRouter 的差异，说明 multi_queue_d_ary_heap 如何让多线程并行扩展堆搜索。

units.title: 共享库、测试与工程实践

lectures.title: 共享库 libvtrutil 与 VPR 工具
lectures.topic: 讲解 libvtrutil 提供的通用数据结构（vtr_vector、vtr_ndmatrix、vtr_cache、日志/错误）以及 vpr_utils 如何在多阶段间共享辅助逻辑。
learning_goals: 掌握 libvtrutil 的核心容器与工具
理解 StrongId 索引容器的设计
了解 vpr_utils 的跨阶段用途
minimal_modules: 通用容器与 ID 容器
lectures.practice_task: 在 libvtrutil/src 中挑出 vtr_vector 与 vtr_ndmatrix，说明它们如何用 StrongId 作下标，并写出 vpr_utils 中一个被多处调用的辅助函数。

lectures.title: 单元测试体系 Catch2
lectures.topic: 讲解 VPR/libarchfpga/libvtrutil 的 Catch2 单元测试组织方式，以及如何构建运行单个测试用例。
learning_goals: 掌握各模块测试二进制的源到目标映射
理解 Catch2 测试的组织与命名
了解如何新增单元测试
minimal_modules: 测试目录与二进制映射
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make && make test，并执行 build/vpr/test_vpr --list-tests 列出连接路由相关的测试用例，挑选一个读懂其断言逻辑。

lectures.title: 回归测试与 QoR 评估
lectures.topic: 讲解 vtr_flow 的回归任务体系（run_vtr_task.py）、黄金结果比对、以及如何用 parse_vtr_task 与 qor_compare 评估质量结果（QoR）。
learning_goals: 掌握回归任务的配置与运行
理解黄金结果的生成与校验
了解 QoR 对比与 PR 流程
lectures.practice_task: 按 testing.md 步骤复制一个 vtr_reg_strong 任务配置，运行并生成黄金结果，再用 qor_compare 对比两次运行的 QoR。
